patterns.matches: Any subdomain of atlassian.net — and the apex domain itself — at any path depth
That exact host, any path below it
A bare host: matches all of its subpaths
* stays inside one path segment; ** or a trailing * crosses segments
Everything — but you don't need it; unmatched links already go to your fallback

actions.body: Open in a browser or app, named by its bundle ID.
Open in a specific browser profile — a Chromium profile directory (Default, Profile 1) or a Firefox profile name (what firefox -P takes, listed in Settings → Browsers).
Rewrite the link to a native app deep link. Works whether or not the rewriter is enabled in Settings → Deep Links.
Show the picker and let you choose at click time. The picker can also be your fallback for every unmatched link: "fallback": { "app": "picker" }.
Copy the link instead of opening it.

menubarActions.label: Recent links
menubarActions.body: the last 10 links Junction routed, in a submenu. In memory only — never written to disk.

menubarActions.label: Settings
menubarActions.body: fallback (a browser, or the ask-every-time picker), rules, browsers and profiles — including which appear in the picker — deep links, updates.

menubarActions.label: Updates
menubarActions.body: a check runs on its own; the icon gets a dot and the menu offers Update to … when one is ready.

troubleshooting.title: macOS refuses to open the app
troubleshooting.body: Junction isn't notarized yet, so macOS quarantines it. Clear the flag with xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine /Applications/Junction.app, or right-click Junction.app → Open → Open.

troubleshooting.title: Nothing routes at all
troubleshooting.body: Junction only sees links when it is the default browser — that's the one step it can't work without. Re-select it under System Settings → Desktop & Dock → Default web browser.

troubleshooting.title: Links open in the wrong place
troubleshooting.body: Almost always precedence: a broader rule above is catching the link first. Run junction test <url> — it prints the matching rule and the decision without opening anything.

troubleshooting.title: A rule opens the fallback instead of its app
troubleshooting.body: Junction degrades to the fallback rather than opening the wrong thing — a deleted browser profile, an app that isn't installed, or an unknown rewriter all land there. The menu-bar icon flags an invalid config, and the last-good rules stay live until you fix it.

troubleshooting.title: Slack deep links open the browser
troubleshooting.body: Slack permalinks don't carry the team ID the app needs. Add the workspace in Settings → Deep Links — type it or paste a Slack link, and Junction reads the ID from the signed-in Slack app; unmapped workspaces open in the browser rather than on the wrong screen.

unsupported: Firefox containers are an extension feature with no launch-flag equivalent — Firefox profiles are supported.
roadmap: MVP under active development. Planned for v1.1+: redirect and shortener unwrapping (opt-in), mailto: routing, time- and network-based conditions, and Shortcuts actions.
